prompt: Could you do a read-only check on the trip “Kyoto research visit” at Kyoto and tell me its record ID and current departure date? This is only a check for now: do not change, cancel, or notify anything. If a requested detail is absent from the returned record, label it unknown instead of guessing.

follow_up_turns.content: That is the right trip. For that same record only, set the departure date to 2026-11-19; keep notifications off.
Small correction: set the departure date to 2026-12-23, not 2026-11-19. This replaces only the value from my last message; keep the same record and do not notify anyone.

stages.purpose: Resolve one exact target by name and location while leaving nearby distractors untouched.
Apply the first requested departure date only to the resolved target.
Replace the superseded departure date on that same target with the user's correction.

response_constraints: Keep every follow-up tied to the record resolved in the preceding stage; do not switch to a similarly named record.
Do not report a lookup or update as successful unless the corresponding tool result establishes it.
After the correction, report the final departure date and make clear that the first requested departure date was superseded.
Label any requested field omitted by a tool result as unknown; do not infer it from the prompt or fixture.
